text.title: Kubernetes Deployment Workloads Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Deployment Workloads`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Workload kind MUST match runtime behaviour: stateless app to Deployment; stable id/ordered to StatefulSet; per-node to DaemonSet; one-shot to Job; scheduled to CronJob.
rule.rationale: Misclassification causes wrong scheduling guarantees: a Deployment cannot give stable network identity, a StatefulSet rolls one-by-one.

rule.statement: StatefulSet MUST declare volumeClaimTemplates; StatefulSets without persistent volumes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: StatefulSet without volumes is a Deployment in disguise + carries the slower rolling guarantee for no benefit.

rule.statement: CronJob MUST set concurrencyPolicy Forbid or Replace unless overlapping runs are explicitly desired.
rule.rationale: Default Allow lets long-running jobs pile up and exhaust the cluster.

rule.statement: Job MUST set backoffLimit and activeDeadlineSeconds; defaults can retry forever.
rule.rationale: Default Job will retry indefinitely; bounded backoff + deadline prevent runaway retries.

rule.statement: DaemonSet MUST declare explicit tolerations for system-taint nodes when running on the control plane / GPU nodes / spot pools.
rule.rationale: Without tolerations DaemonSet skips tainted nodes; coverage gaps follow.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
